summary: Remote code execution backdoor: fetches and eval()s attacker-controlled payload from jsonkeeper.com
details: The package advertises itself as an SVG fetcher/sanitizer but ships a hidden remote-code-execution path. index.js exports getPlugin(), which returns a function that issues an HTTP GET against https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/NGY3C — an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host — and passes the response's `parsed.model` field directly to eval() in the consuming process. Errors are silently swallowed. Any consumer that invokes getPlugin()() executes whatever JavaScript the paste currently contains, giving the operator of the paste arbitrary code execution in the installer's runtime. The package name (`2fa-exe`) bears no relation to the advertised SVG functionality and appears designed to attract users searching for two-factor-authentication tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
